title: [ORC] Implement scheduled command injection (svf_command_schedule mark)
body:
## Objective
Allow test procedures to schedule commands at specific simulation times rather than wall-clock times. Required for multi-phase tests where the simulation runs faster than real time.

## Problem
SoftwareTickSource runs all simulation steps in milliseconds. Mid-test inject() calls fire into an already-completed simulation, making multi-phase test procedures unreliable.

## Design
- SimulationMaster writes current simulation time to ParameterStore as 'svf.sim_time'
- New pytest mark: @pytest.mark.svf_command_schedule([(t, name, value), ...])
- Fixture monitors 'svf.sim_time' from ParameterStore and fires commands when t is reached
- Commands fired in a background thread that polls sim_time during the simulation run

## Acceptance Criteria
- svf_command_schedule mark accepted by svf_session fixture
- Commands fired at correct simulation time (within one dt)
- TC-PWR-003 and TC-PWR-005 rewritten to use scheduled commands
- All EPS tests pass
- mypy clean

## Implements
SVF-DEV-042
